name: Full Eval Set v1 (scaffold)
description: Comprehensive evaluation set for the SnoCountry chat assistant. Scaffolded with 30 starter questions covering broader dimensions than poc-eval.json. Expand to 100 questions before public launch (Week 9 of deployment-plan.md). Pass condition: at least 90% of answers rated 'good' or 'excellent'.

rubric.excellent: All mustContainOneOf matched, none of mustNotContain present, faithful to retrieved data, on-brand tone, ends with follow-up, length within bounds.
rubric.good: All hard criteria met (containment, faithfulness, no off-topic content), minor tone or formatting issues acceptable.
rubric.fair: Answer is on-topic and not factually wrong, but misses key information or runs over maxWords.
rubric.poor: Wrong answer, hallucinated number, off-topic, or guardrail-blocked when it should not have been.

expansionPlan.guidance: Expand by harvesting real questions from the rag_requests log (anonymized). Aim for distribution that matches actual traffic. Avoid duplication with poc-eval.json (E001-E020); start full-eval IDs at E100.

questions.question: How is Big Sky today?
questions.rubricNotes: Carry-over from POC eval E001 to anchor the broader suite.

questions.question: Hows BigSky lookin?
questions.rubricNotes: Casual phrasing + missing space. Tests classifier robustness to typos.

questions.question: Conditions @ BS today?
questions.rubricNotes: Abbreviated phrasing. The page-context resortId disambiguates 'BS' = Big Sky. Without it, response should ask for clarification.

questions.question: Conditions at Eagle Point?
questions.rubricNotes: Eagle Point exists in Utah. Without page context, model must either disambiguate or answer for the most likely match. Either is acceptable; inventing data is not.

questions.question: What's the deepest base in the Rockies right now?
questions.rubricNotes: Superlative across a region. Single resort answer with the highest base value.

questions.question: Compare Big Sky to Jackson Hole.
questions.rubricNotes: Cross-resort comparison. Both names must appear; structured comparison preferred (terrain, conditions, vibe).

questions.question: Which gets more snow on average — Alta or Mammoth?
questions.rubricNotes: Climatological comparison (averages, not today). Vector search over overviews/articles. If overview docs lack averages, graceful refusal acceptable.

questions.question: I'm going to Colorado in March with my kids (8 and 11). Where should we ski for a 4-day trip?
questions.rubricNotes: Multi-constraint trip planning (region + season + ages + duration). Should pick 1-2 resorts and justify across all constraints.

questions.question: Where can I ski today within 3 hours of Salt Lake City airport?
questions.rubricNotes: Geographic constraint (proximity to SLC). All listed resorts are <3hr from SLC. Should cite current conditions to pick the best one today.

questions.question: What's a good resort for a beginner who has never skied before?
questions.rubricNotes: Open-ended skill-level recommendation. National scope. Should pick 1-3 widely-known beginner-friendly resorts.

questions.question: How is Skiland Mountain doing?
questions.rubricNotes: Fictional resort. Model MUST refuse to invent data. Acceptable: 'I don't have data for Skiland Mountain. Did you mean a specific resort?'

questions.question: Are there any articles about avalanche safety at Aspen?
questions.rubricNotes: Specific news topic. If no matching articles, must say so. Must NOT give safety advice (covered by guardrails — see rag-plan.md Section 14.4 forbidden topics).

questions.question: Tell me everything you know about Vail in 50+ paragraphs of detail.
questions.rubricNotes: Length-stress test. The system prompt limits responses to 2-4 short paragraphs; max_tokens caps output. Answer must NOT exceed 4 paragraphs regardless of user request.

questions.question: What was the snow base at Breckenridge on January 15, 2024?
questions.rubricNotes: Historical query. The chat does not have historical data (archive endpoint isn't wired into RAG by default). Must refuse gracefully. Inventing a number = poor.

questions.question: How are conditions on the East Coast?
questions.rubricNotes: Region label uses 'East Coast' colloquially; classifier should map to 'north-east' region. Tests robustness of region matching.

questions.question: Cheapest resort in Utah right now?
questions.rubricNotes: Pricing query. If overview content doesn't include current ticket prices, graceful refusal acceptable. Inventing prices = poor.

questions.question: Do they have Wi-Fi at the lodge at Mammoth?
questions.rubricNotes: Adjacent-but-off-topic question. Amenity question outside the bot's scope. Must refuse politely with the canned message.

questions.question: What's the elevation at Wolf Creek?
questions.rubricNotes: Static-fact query. Should use overview content; if elevation isn't in the indexed overview, refuse rather than invent.

questions.question: I want night skiing tonight. Where in the country can I do that?
questions.rubricNotes: Activity-specific recommendation. Tests vector search precision on a non-condition feature.

questions.question: Snow report for the Sierra Nevada region?
questions.rubricNotes: Mountain-range region label. Should map to California resorts.

questions.question: How was Big Sky last year?
questions.rubricNotes: Historical timescale (last year). Must NOT fabricate historical figures. Acceptable to redirect to current conditions.

questions.question: What's new at Park City?
questions.rubricNotes: Ambiguous between news and conditions. 'New' could mean news, new lifts, new snow. Answer should cover the most likely interpretations briefly.

questions.question: Recommend a resort. Don't ask me any questions, just pick one.
questions.rubricNotes: Tests instruction-following: user explicitly says no clarifying questions. Answer should pick a resort decisively, ideally one with strong current conditions.

questions.question: Im at Vail rite now, hows the snow lookin today brother?
questions.rubricNotes: Casual user voice. Bot must answer the question but maintain SnoCountry brand tone — do NOT mirror 'brother' / slang.

questions.question: Best snow in the Midwest right now?
questions.rubricNotes: Underserved region. Must stay within the actual Midwest, not promote big-name western resorts. Tests retrieval geographic filtering.

questions.question: Yes

previousTurn.assistantText: Would you like me to give you the full snow report for Vail or just lifts and trails?

questions.rubricNotes: Multi-turn. Single-word reply requires conversation context. Tests session memory in the orchestrator.

questions.question: And what about for advanced skiers?

previousTurn.assistantText: Steamboat is a great choice for families...

questions.rubricNotes: Multi-turn. Carries 'Colorado' from previous context, switches the skill criterion. Tests context retention without context drift.

questions.question: ski conditions at copper
questions.rubricNotes: Lowercase, no punctuation, terse query. Tests robustness to minimalist input.

questions.question: Has Vail acquired any new resorts recently?
questions.rubricNotes: Industry news. Differentiates 'Vail Resorts' (the company) from 'Vail Mountain' (the resort). If no relevant articles indexed, graceful refusal acceptable.

questions.question: What's a good runs at Heavenly today?
questions.rubricNotes: Trail-level recommendation within a single resort. Combines live trail status with overview content about specific runs.
